name: Oruka Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Oruka Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing novel monoclonal antibody therapeutics for psoriasis and other inflammatory and immunology indications. Its lead programs target IL-23p19 (ORKA-001) and IL-17A/F (ORKA-002) for conditions such as moderate-to-severe psoriasis, hidradenitis suppurativa, and psoriatic arthritis.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel monoclonal antibody therapeutics for psoriasis (PsO) and other inflammatory and immunology (I&I) indications.

catalysts.event: Week 16 data readout from EVERLAST-A Phase 2a trial of ORKA-001
catalysts.sentence: We expect to share Week 16 data for all patients in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Longer-term data from EVERLAST-A including Week 28 and 52-week follow-up
catalysts.sentence: In addition, we plan to share longer-term data, including Week 28 for all patients and 52-week follow-up for a portion of the cohort in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Data from EVERLAST-B Phase 2b trial of ORKA-001
catalysts.sentence: Data from EVERLAST-B is anticipated in 2027.

catalysts.event: Data from ORCA-SURGE Phase 2 trial of ORKA-002 in psoriasis
catalysts.sentence: Data from ORCA-SURGE is anticipated in 2027.

catalysts.event: Initiation of Phase 2 trial of ORKA-002 in hidradenitis suppurativa
catalysts.sentence: Moreover, we also expect to initiate a Phase 2 trial of ORKA-002 in patients with HS in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Psoriasis affects 125 million people worldwide and is the largest pharmaceutical market within dermatology, with annual global sales of approximately $28 billion in 2024, estimated to grow to $40 billion by 2032.
tamClaims.sentence: PsO is a chronic autoimmune skin disorder that affects an estimated 125 million people worldwide with steadily increasing prevalence, estimated to be around 2-3% of the population currently, according to the International Federation of Psoriasis Associations (IFPA). It is the largest pharmaceutical market within dermatology, with annual global sales of approximately $28 billion in 2024, which is estimated to grow to approximately $40 billion by 2032.
